name: Commonwealth of Nations

colors.symbolism: the oceans linking the member states of the Commonwealth
the sun whose rays radiate to all member states

description: A blue field charged at the centre with the gold Commonwealth emblem: a globe encircled by a halo of slender golden rays radiating outward.
history: The Commonwealth flag was unveiled on 26 March 1976 at the Commonwealth Secretariat in London, replacing the unofficial use of the British flag. The original design had a 'C' formed by the rays around the globe; the design was revised in 2013 to remove the C-shape gap, producing a continuous halo of rays around the globe. The Commonwealth of Nations was formally constituted by the London Declaration of 28 April 1949.

features.symbolism: Commonwealth blue, evoking the seas across which the member states are spread

features.role: the Commonwealth emblem (globe encircled by spear-like rays)
features.symbolism: A central golden globe representing the Earth, encircled by golden rays. The rays do not represent individual members; the radiating sun symbolises the global reach of the Commonwealth.
